main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Attention roll-out mechanism for explainability in transformer-based audio deepfake detectors","Conceptual explainability framework for deepfake audio detection","Generalizability benchmark evaluating cross-dataset performance (train on ASVspoof 5, test on FakeAVCeleb)","Empirical evaluation and comparison of various explainability methods for audio transformers"],"problems":["Lack of explainability in current AI-driven audio deepfake detection solutions","Poor generalization of deepfake detectors to real-world, diverse, and unseen audio samples","Gap between controlled benchmark performance and real-world applicability in audio deepfake detection"],"datasets":["ASVspoof 5","FakeAVCeleb"],"metrics":["Generalization performance (cross-dataset accuracy/performance)","Explainability (qualitative and conceptual evaluation)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of a novel attention roll-out mechanism to improve explainability for transformer-based audio deepfake detectors","Development of a conceptual explainability framework tailored for deepfake audio detection","Creation and open-sourcing of a new generalizability benchmark for audio deepfake classifiers, evaluating models trained on ASVspoof 5 and tested on FakeAVCeleb","Comprehensive empirical evaluation and comparison of explainability methods for audio transformers in the context of deepfake detection"]}

methods: Attention roll-out mechanism for explainability in transformer-based audio deepfake detectors

problems: Lack of explainability in current AI-driven audio deepfake detection solutions

novelty_claims: Introduction of a novel attention roll-out mechanism to improve explainability for transformer-based audio deepfake detectors
Development of a conceptual explainability framework tailored for deepfake audio detection

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Neurosymbolic deepfake detection framework","Inter- and intra-modality reasoning on emotions","Extraction of emotions from audio and visual modalities","Use of psychological and arousal-valence model for emotion analysis","Generation of textual explanations for detection decisions"],"problems":["Vulnerability of deepfake detectors to unseen deepfakes due to training on specific datasets","Lack of explainability in existing deepfake detection methods"],"datasets":["Presidential Deepfakes Dataset","World Leaders Dataset"],"metrics":["Detection effectiveness (e.g., accuracy, though not explicitly stated)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a neurosymbolic framework for deepfake detection that combines symbolic reasoning with neural emotion extraction.","Performs inter- and intra-modality reasoning on emotional expressions from both audio and visual modalities.","Utilizes a psychological and arousal-valence model for emotion analysis in deepfake detection.","Provides textual explanations for deepfake detection decisions, enhancing explainability."]}

Use of psychological and arousal-valence model for emotion analysis

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Pipeline-based audio deepfake detection (feature extraction + classifier)","End-to-end audio deepfake detection (joint feature extraction and classification on raw audio)","Unified comparison of representative features and classifiers"],"problems":["Detection of audio deepfakes (distinguishing genuine from fake utterances)","Lack of systematic overview and unified evaluation of audio deepfake detection methods","Poor generalization of existing detection methods to unknown fake attacks","Lack of large-scale in-the-wild datasets for audio deepfake detection","Interpretability of detection results"],"datasets":["ASVspoof 2021","ADD 2023","In-the-Wild"],"metrics":["Accuracy","Other standard evaluation metrics for audio deepfake detection (not explicitly listed, but implied)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First comprehensive survey providing a systematic overview of audio deepfake detection developments with unified evaluation.","Provides a unified comparison of representative features and classifiers on multiple benchmark datasets (ASVspoof 2021, ADD 2023, In-the-Wild).","Highlights key differences across various types of deepfake audio and analyzes competitions, datasets, features, classifications, and evaluation of state-of-the-art approaches.","Discusses major challenges and future research directions in audio deepfake detection."]}

methods: Pipeline-based audio deepfake detection (feature extraction + classifier)
End-to-end audio deepfake detection (joint feature extraction and classification on raw audio)
Unified comparison of representative features and classifiers
problems: Detection of audio deepfakes (distinguishing genuine from fake utterances)
Lack of systematic overview and unified evaluation of audio deepfake detection methods
Poor generalization of existing detection methods to unknown fake attacks

Highlights key differences across various types of deepfake audio and analyzes competitions, datasets, features, classifications, and evaluation of state-of-the-art approaches.
Discusses major challenges and future research directions in audio deepfake detection.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Contrastive Learning-based Audio deepfake Detector (CLAD)","Contrastive learning for robust audio encoding","Length loss for clustering real audio features","Systematic evaluation of manipulation attacks (noise injection, volume control, fading, time stretching, resampling, time shifting, echoes adding)"],"problems":["Vulnerability of audio deepfake detectors to simple audio manipulation attacks","Lack of robustness in existing audio deepfake detection methods against post-synthesis manipulations","Need for robust detection of audio deepfakes in the presence of realistic adversarial manipulations"],"datasets":["ASVspoof 2021 dataset"],"metrics":["False Acceptance Rate (FAR)"],"results":[{"metric":"FAR (existing detectors, volume control)","value":"36.69%"},{"metric":"FAR (existing detectors, fading)","value":"31.23%"},{"metric":"FAR (existing detectors, noise injection)","value":"51.28%"},{"metric":"FAR (CLAD, noise injection)","value":"0.81%"},{"metric":"FAR (CLAD, all manipulations)","value":"<1.63%"}],"novelty_claims":["First comprehensive study of the robustness of widely adopted audio deepfake detectors against manipulation attacks.","First audio deepfake detection method (CLAD) robust to manipulation attacks using contrastive learning.","Introduction of length loss as a new learning strategy to improve clustering of real audio features and enhance detection accuracy.","Demonstration that CLAD achieves significantly lower FAR under manipulation attacks compared to existing methods."]}

methods: Contrastive Learning-based Audio deepfake Detector (CLAD)
Contrastive learning for robust audio encoding
Length loss for clustering real audio features
Systematic evaluation of manipulation attacks (noise injection, volume control, fading, time stretching, resampling, time shifting, echoes adding)
problems: Vulnerability of audio deepfake detectors to simple audio manipulation attacks
Lack of robustness in existing audio deepfake detection methods against post-synthesis manipulations
Need for robust detection of audio deepfakes in the presence of realistic adversarial manipulations
novelty_claims: First comprehensive study of the robustness of widely adopted audio deepfake detectors against manipulation attacks.
First audio deepfake detection method (CLAD) robust to manipulation attacks using contrastive learning.
Introduction of length loss as a new learning strategy to improve clustering of real audio features and enhance detection accuracy.
Demonstration that CLAD achieves significantly lower FAR under manipulation attacks compared to existing methods.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Automated manipulation pipeline for generating deepfake general audio (grounding, regeneration, super-resolution)","Creation of the FakeSound dataset for deepfake general audio detection","Deepfake detection model utilizing a general audio pre-trained model as a benchmark system"],"problems":["Detection of deepfake general audio (not limited to speech, includes environmental sounds, etc.)","Localization of manipulated (fake) regions within audio content","Lack of datasets and benchmarks for general audio deepfake detection"],"datasets":["FakeSound (proposed in this paper)"],"metrics":["Binary accuracy (human and model)"],"results":[{"metric":"Human binary accuracy (all test sets)","value":"< 0.6"},{"metric":"Proposed model performance","value":"Surpasses state-of-the-art in deepfake speech detection and human testers (exact value not specified)"}],"novelty_claims":["First to propose the task of deepfake general audio detection (beyond speech)","Introduction of the FakeSound dataset for deepfake general audio detection, covering a wide range of audio types and manipulation scenarios","Development of an automated pipeline for generating challenging deepfake general audio, including 'half-truth' inpainting cases","Demonstration that humans perform poorly on this task, highlighting its difficulty and the need for automated solutions","Proposed a benchmark detection model that outperforms state-of-the-art speech deepfake detectors and human evaluators on the FakeSound dataset"]}

methods: Automated manipulation pipeline for generating deepfake general audio (grounding, regeneration, super-resolution)
Creation of the FakeSound dataset for deepfake general audio detection
Deepfake detection model utilizing a general audio pre-trained model as a benchmark system
problems: Detection of deepfake general audio (not limited to speech, includes environmental sounds, etc.)
Localization of manipulated (fake) regions within audio content
Lack of datasets and benchmarks for general audio deepfake detection

results.value: Surpasses state-of-the-art in deepfake speech detection and human testers (exact value not specified)

novelty_claims: First to propose the task of deepfake general audio detection (beyond speech)
Introduction of the FakeSound dataset for deepfake general audio detection, covering a wide range of audio types and manipulation scenarios
Development of an automated pipeline for generating challenging deepfake general audio, including 'half-truth' inpainting cases
Demonstration that humans perform poorly on this task, highlighting its difficulty and the need for automated solutions
Proposed a benchmark detection model that outperforms state-of-the-art speech deepfake detectors and human evaluators on the FakeSound dataset

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Creation of FakeAVCeleb: a multimodal dataset containing both deepfake videos and corresponding synthesized, lip-synced fake audios using popular deepfake generation methods","Selection of real YouTube videos of celebrities from four/five major ethnic backgrounds to address racial bias","Generation of three different types of audio-video deepfakes","Comprehensive evaluation using 11 different unimodal, ensemble-based, and multimodal baseline deepfake detection methods"],"problems":["Lack of high-quality datasets containing both deepfake videos and corresponding fake audios for multimodal deepfake detection","Existing datasets are racially biased and do not represent diverse ethnic backgrounds","Current datasets do not provide fine-grained labels for audio and video fakeness, hindering the development of multimodal deepfake detectors","Need for datasets that enable the development and evaluation of detectors that can handle both audio and video deepfakes simultaneously"],"datasets":["FakeAVCeleb (proposed)","Deepfake Detection Challenge (DFDC)","Other popular deepfake datasets (not named specifically in the excerpt, but referenced in the evaluation)"],"metrics":["Detection accuracy (implied from evaluation of detection methods)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["FakeAVCeleb is the first multimodal deepfake dataset containing both deepfake videos and corresponding synthesized, lip-synced fake audios with fine-grained labels for each modality","FakeAVCeleb addresses racial bias by including individuals from five major ethnic backgrounds with balanced gender and age representation","FakeAVCeleb enables the development and evaluation of multimodal deepfake detectors, which was not possible with previous datasets","The dataset contains three different types of audio-video deepfakes generated using state-of-the-art methods"]}

methods: Creation of FakeAVCeleb: a multimodal dataset containing both deepfake videos and corresponding synthesized, lip-synced fake audios using popular deepfake generation methods
Selection of real YouTube videos of celebrities from four/five major ethnic backgrounds to address racial bias
Generation of three different types of audio-video deepfakes
Comprehensive evaluation using 11 different unimodal, ensemble-based, and multimodal baseline deepfake detection methods
problems: Lack of high-quality datasets containing both deepfake videos and corresponding fake audios for multimodal deepfake detection
Existing datasets are racially biased and do not represent diverse ethnic backgrounds
Current datasets do not provide fine-grained labels for audio and video fakeness, hindering the development of multimodal deepfake detectors
Need for datasets that enable the development and evaluation of detectors that can handle both audio and video deepfakes simultaneously
datasets: Other popular deepfake datasets (not named specifically in the excerpt, but referenced in the evaluation)
metrics: Detection accuracy (implied from evaluation of detection methods)
novelty_claims: FakeAVCeleb is the first multimodal deepfake dataset containing both deepfake videos and corresponding synthesized, lip-synced fake audios with fine-grained labels for each modality
FakeAVCeleb addresses racial bias by including individuals from five major ethnic backgrounds with balanced gender and age representation
FakeAVCeleb enables the development and evaluation of multimodal deepfake detectors, which was not possible with previous datasets
The dataset contains three different types of audio-video deepfakes generated using state-of-the-art methods

Pretrained feature extractor processes both the input image and its corresponding XAI image
Feature embeddings from both images are used to train a classifier to detect adversarial attacks on deepfake detectors
problems: Vulnerability of deepfake detectors to adversarial attacks
Lack of effective mechanisms to detect adversarial attacks on deepfake detectors
Need for increased transparency and interpretability in adversarial detection for deepfake systems
novelty_claims: Introduction of a novel XAI-based methodology for detecting adversarial attacks on deepfake detectors

Demonstration of robustness against both familiar and previously unseen adversarial attacks

methods: Audio Spectrogram Transformer (AST) for audio deepfake detection
Large-scale collection of audio deepfake data from over 50 open-source speech generation models (TTS, VC, audio LLMs)
Data augmentation to simulate compression, far-field, noise, and distortions
Continuous learning plugin module for model updating with few-shot labeled data
Two-stage updating: (1) discriminative learning with AST embeddings and gradient boost machine for few-shot adaptation, (2) conventional fine-tuning after collecting more new fake samples
problems: Accurate detection of audio deepfakes across diverse and challenging conditions
Continuous learning to adapt to new types of audio deepfakes with minimal labeled data
Improving robustness to open-set and real-world variations (compression, noise, far-field, etc.)
Overcoming limitations of supervised and challenge-driven approaches that do not generalize well to new/unseen fake types
datasets: Large-scale collected dataset (>2 million fake samples from 50+ sources)
Various public benchmark datasets (not named specifically in the excerpt)

results.metric: AUC (unseen datasets, after few-shot continuous learning, 0.1% labeled data)
AUC (unseen datasets, after unsupervised detection and fine-tuning)

novelty_claims: First to propose a continuous learning framework for audio deepfake detection that can adapt to new fake types with very few labeled samples.
Large-scale collection and augmentation of audio deepfake data from a wide variety of sources, increasing robustness.

Novel two-stage continuous learning plugin: (1) discriminative learning with AST embeddings and gradient boost machine for few-shot adaptation, (2) conventional fine-tuning after more data is available.
Demonstrates significant improvement over conventional fine-tuning approaches in adapting to new fake types with minimal labeled data.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Systematic literature review of deepfake detectors (2019-2023)","Development of a unified conceptual framework categorizing detectors by 18 key factors","Categorization of 51 top deepfake detectors into 4 high-level groups and 13 sub-groups","Rigorous evaluation framework for detector generalizability (black-box, gray-box, white-box settings)","Creation of the first 'white-box' deepfake dataset for controlled evaluation","Comprehensive assessment of 16 state-of-the-art detectors across diverse attack scenarios"],"problems":["Lack of generalizability of deepfake detectors to real-world, unseen deepfakes","Over-reliance on lab-generated datasets for detector validation","Insufficient systematic evaluation and categorization of deepfake detectors and generation tools","Limited understanding of factors influencing detector efficacy and generalizability","Gap between reported detector performance and real-world effectiveness"],"datasets":["Novel 'white-box' deepfake dataset (created in this work)","Standard academic deepfake datasets (e.g., FaceForensics++, DeepFakeDetection, etc. as referenced, but not explicitly named in the provided text)"],"metrics":["Generalizability (across black-box, gray-box, white-box settings)","Detection accuracy","Robustness to noise and compression","Performance on unseen deepfakes"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First comprehensive systematization and benchmarking of deepfake detectors in a unified framework","Introduction of a conceptual framework categorizing detectors by 18 influential factors","Creation of the first 'white-box' deepfake dataset for controlled evaluation","Comprehensive evaluation of 16 state-of-the-art detectors across black-box, gray-box, and white-box scenarios","Identification and analysis of factors impacting detector generalizability","Provision of practical insights and future directions for deepfake detection research"]}

Comprehensive assessment of 16 state-of-the-art detectors across diverse attack scenarios

novelty_claims: First comprehensive systematization and benchmarking of deepfake detectors in a unified framework
Introduction of a conceptual framework categorizing detectors by 18 influential factors
Creation of the first 'white-box' deepfake dataset for controlled evaluation

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Construction of a new cross-domain audio deepfake detection (ADD) dataset (CD-ADD) with over 300 hours of speech data generated by five advanced zero-shot TTS models","Attack-augmented training: training detection models with data including nine different attack types (including DNN-based codecs and noise reduction)","Evaluation of Wav2Vec2-large and Whisper-medium models for ADD","Few-shot fine-tuning: fine-tuning detection models with just one minute of target-domain data","Pairwise cross-model performance analysis and use of audio prompts from different domains for cross-domain evaluation"],"problems":["Outdated and limited existing ADD datasets that do not include advanced zero-shot TTS models","Lack of transparency and diversity in attack types in current datasets, especially missing DNN-based attacks","Suboptimal generalization of current audio deepfake detection models across domains and models","Need for robust ADD models that can generalize to new, unseen TTS models and attacks"],"datasets":["CD-ADD (Cross-Domain Audio Deepfake Detection) dataset (introduced in this paper)"],"metrics":["Equal Error Rate (EER)"],"results":[{"metric":"Equal Error Rate (EER) for Wav2Vec2-large","value":"4.1%"},{"metric":"Equal Error Rate (EER) for Whisper-medium","value":"6.5%"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of a large-scale, cross-domain ADD dataset (CD-ADD) with over 300 hours of speech from five advanced zero-shot TTS models","Inclusion of nine diverse attack types, including DNN-based codecs and noise reduction, in the dataset","Novel cross-domain evaluation setup using pairwise cross-model performance and audio prompts from different domains","Demonstration of strong few-shot ADD performance with only one minute of target-domain data","Public release of the CD-ADD dataset"]}

methods: Construction of a new cross-domain audio deepfake detection (ADD) dataset (CD-ADD) with over 300 hours of speech data generated by five advanced zero-shot TTS models
Attack-augmented training: training detection models with data including nine different attack types (including DNN-based codecs and noise reduction)
Evaluation of Wav2Vec2-large and Whisper-medium models for ADD
Few-shot fine-tuning: fine-tuning detection models with just one minute of target-domain data
Pairwise cross-model performance analysis and use of audio prompts from different domains for cross-domain evaluation
problems: Outdated and limited existing ADD datasets that do not include advanced zero-shot TTS models
Lack of transparency and diversity in attack types in current datasets, especially missing DNN-based attacks
Suboptimal generalization of current audio deepfake detection models across domains and models
Need for robust ADD models that can generalize to new, unseen TTS models and attacks
datasets: CD-ADD (Cross-Domain Audio Deepfake Detection) dataset (introduced in this paper)
novelty_claims: Introduction of a large-scale, cross-domain ADD dataset (CD-ADD) with over 300 hours of speech from five advanced zero-shot TTS models
Inclusion of nine diverse attack types, including DNN-based codecs and noise reduction, in the dataset
Novel cross-domain evaluation setup using pairwise cross-model performance and audio prompts from different domains
Demonstration of strong few-shot ADD performance with only one minute of target-domain data
Public release of the CD-ADD dataset

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Use of off-the-shelf self-supervised learning (SSL) based speech foundation models for feature extraction without fine-tuning","Application of classical machine learning algorithms (e.g., logistic regression, shallow neural networks) on SSL embeddings for audio deepfake detection","Exploration of layer-wise representations from speech foundation models, especially using initial layers to reduce parameters and computation"],"problems":["High carbon footprint and energy consumption of state-of-the-art deep learning models for audio deepfake detection","Need for eco-friendly (Green AI), computationally and memory-efficient audio deepfake detection methods that can be trained and run on standard CPU resources"],"datasets":["ASVspoof 2019 LA"],"metrics":["Equal Error Rate (EER)","Number of trained model parameters"],"results":[{"metric":"Equal Error Rate (EER)","value":"0.90%"},{"metric":"Number of trained model parameters","value":"<1k"}],"novelty_claims":["First study to investigate the use of classical machine learning techniques (such as logistic regression and shallow neural networks) alongside speech foundation models for audio deepfake detection","Proposes a Green AI framework for audio deepfake detection that does not require high-performance computing resources and can be trained and run on a single CPU","Demonstrates that competitive results can be achieved using initial layer representations from speech foundation models and classical ML algorithms, with very few parameters and low computational cost"]}

methods: Use of off-the-shelf self-supervised learning (SSL) based speech foundation models for feature extraction without fine-tuning
Application of classical machine learning algorithms (e.g., logistic regression, shallow neural networks) on SSL embeddings for audio deepfake detection
Exploration of layer-wise representations from speech foundation models, especially using initial layers to reduce parameters and computation
problems: High carbon footprint and energy consumption of state-of-the-art deep learning models for audio deepfake detection
Need for eco-friendly (Green AI), computationally and memory-efficient audio deepfake detection methods that can be trained and run on standard CPU resources
novelty_claims: First study to investigate the use of classical machine learning techniques (such as logistic regression and shallow neural networks) alongside speech foundation models for audio deepfake detection
Proposes a Green AI framework for audio deepfake detection that does not require high-performance computing resources and can be trained and run on a single CPU

Demonstrates that competitive results can be achieved using initial layer representations from speech foundation models and classical ML algorithms, with very few parameters and low computational cost

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Frame-level boundary detection system for partially spoofed audio","Deep learning-based detection using Wav2Vec feature extractor","ResNet-1D as main framework","Transformer encoder-based frame-level backend classifier","Utilization of discontinuity between segments for boundary detection"],"problems":["Detection of partially spoofed/fake audio where only segments of an utterance are manipulated","Locating the manipulated (fake) segments within an audio utterance","Improving detection performance over utterance-level binary classification approaches"],"datasets":["ADD2022 Challenge dataset (Audio Deep Synthesis Detection Challenge)","Simulated data from ADD2022 Challenge datasets"],"metrics":["Equal Error Rate (EER)"],"results":[{"metric":"EER on ADD adaptation set","value":"3.14%"},{"metric":"EER on ADD2022 challenge test set","value":"6.58%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a frame-level boundary detection system for partially spoofed audio, as opposed to previous utterance-level approaches","System can both detect and locate manipulated segments within an utterance","Utilizes acoustic discontinuity between concatenated segments for boundary detection","Achieves state-of-the-art performance (best EER) for partially spoofed audio detection with localization capability"]}

methods: Frame-level boundary detection system for partially spoofed audio
Deep learning-based detection using Wav2Vec feature extractor
ResNet-1D as main framework
Transformer encoder-based frame-level backend classifier
Utilization of discontinuity between segments for boundary detection
problems: Detection of partially spoofed/fake audio where only segments of an utterance are manipulated
Locating the manipulated (fake) segments within an audio utterance
Improving detection performance over utterance-level binary classification approaches
datasets: ADD2022 Challenge dataset (Audio Deep Synthesis Detection Challenge)
Simulated data from ADD2022 Challenge datasets

results.metric: EER on ADD2022 challenge test set

novelty_claims: Proposes a frame-level boundary detection system for partially spoofed audio, as opposed to previous utterance-level approaches
System can both detect and locate manipulated segments within an utterance
Utilizes acoustic discontinuity between concatenated segments for boundary detection
Achieves state-of-the-art performance (best EER) for partially spoofed audio detection with localization capability

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Use of self-supervised WavLM model as a front-end feature extractor for audio deepfake detection","Proposed Multi-Fusion Attentive (MFA) classifier based on Attentive Statistics Pooling (ASP) layer","Aggregation of WavLM output representations at different layers and time steps via MFA"],"problems":["Detection of audio deepfakes (spoofed audio) that threaten Automatic Speaker Verification (ASV) systems","Improving the effectiveness of deepfake detection by leveraging speaker-related features, which are often neglected by existing self-supervised models"],"datasets":["ASVspoof 2021 DF set","ASVspoof 2019 LA set","ASVspoof 2021 LA set"],"metrics":["Equal Error Rate (EER)","t-DCF (tandem Detection Cost Function)"],"results":[{"metric":"ASVspoof 2021 DF set EER","value":"state-of-the-art (exact value not specified)"},{"metric":"ASVspoof 2019 LA set EER","value":"competitive (exact value not specified)"},{"metric":"ASVspoof 2021 LA set EER","value":"competitive (exact value not specified)"}],"novelty_claims":["First use of the WavLM self-supervised model as a front-end feature extractor for audio deepfake detection","Proposal of a novel Multi-Fusion Attentive (MFA) classifier that captures complementary information at both time and layer levels from WavLM features","Demonstration that combining WavLM and MFA achieves state-of-the-art results on the ASVspoof 2021 DF set"]}

methods: Use of self-supervised WavLM model as a front-end feature extractor for audio deepfake detection
Proposed Multi-Fusion Attentive (MFA) classifier based on Attentive Statistics Pooling (ASP) layer
Aggregation of WavLM output representations at different layers and time steps via MFA
problems: Detection of audio deepfakes (spoofed audio) that threaten Automatic Speaker Verification (ASV) systems
Improving the effectiveness of deepfake detection by leveraging speaker-related features, which are often neglected by existing self-supervised models
novelty_claims: First use of the WavLM self-supervised model as a front-end feature extractor for audio deepfake detection
Proposal of a novel Multi-Fusion Attentive (MFA) classifier that captures complementary information at both time and layer levels from WavLM features

methods: Extraction of representations from state-of-the-art pre-trained models (PTMs) including multilingual, monolingual, speaker recognition, and emotion recognition models
Evaluation of PTM representations with simple downstream networks (Fully Connected Network, Convolutional Neural Network) for audio deepfake detection

State-of-the-art (SOTA) performance achieved with MiO framework
Comparable to current SOTA works with MiO framework

novelty_claims: First comprehensive empirical study demonstrating the superior performance of multilingual PTMs for audio deepfake detection across three benchmark datasets
First to explore and propose fusion of PTM representations for audio deepfake detection via the MiO (Merge into One) framework
MiO framework achieves SOTA performance in terms of Equal Error Rate (EER) on ASV and ITW datasets and competitive performance on DECRO

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Multi-Scale Permutation Entropy (MPE) for audio deepfake detection","Combination of MPE with LFCC features"],"problems":["Detection of audio deepfakes (fake audio attacks) in Automatic Speaker Verification (ASV) systems","Improving generalization and robustness of audio deepfake detection methods"],"datasets":["ASVspoof2019 LA test set","In-the-Wild dataset"],"metrics":["Equal Error Rate (EER)"],"results":[{"metric":"EER (ASVspoof2019 LA test set)","value":"<2% (about 50% lower than LFCC)"},{"metric":"EER (In-the-Wild dataset)","value":"Comparable to Wav2vec, without requiring pretraining"}],"novelty_claims":["First application of multi-scale permutation entropy (MPE) to audio deepfake detection.","Demonstrates that MPE can significantly improve the performance of traditional features like LFCC.","Shows that MPE achieves strong generalization performance comparable to state-of-the-art methods like Wav2vec, but without the need for pretraining."]}

methods: Multi-Scale Permutation Entropy (MPE) for audio deepfake detection
Combination of MPE with LFCC features
problems: Detection of audio deepfakes (fake audio attacks) in Automatic Speaker Verification (ASV) systems
Improving generalization and robustness of audio deepfake detection methods

results.value: Comparable to Wav2vec, without requiring pretraining

novelty_claims: First application of multi-scale permutation entropy (MPE) to audio deepfake detection.
Demonstrates that MPE can significantly improve the performance of traditional features like LFCC.
Shows that MPE achieves strong generalization performance comparable to state-of-the-art methods like Wav2vec, but without the need for pretraining.
